sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to the fruit of commercial varieties of carambolas grown from Averrhoa carambola L., of the Oxalidaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Carambolas for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to the fruit of commercial varieties of carambolas grown from Averrhoa carambola L., of the Oxalidaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Carambolas for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to the fruit of commercial varieties of carambolas grown from Averrhoa carambola L., of the Oxalidaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Carambolas for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Carambolas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety, well-formed and free of blemishes, with the exception of very slight superficial defects in the skin and ribs due to rubbing and bruises, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > CLASSIFICATION > “Extra” Class
number=2.2.1
level=3
Carambolas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety, well-formed and free of blemishes, with the exception of very slight superficial defects in the skin and ribs due to rubbing and bruises, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Carambolas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety, well-formed and free of blemishes, with the exception of very slight superficial defects in the skin and ribs due to rubbing and bruises, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text: Carambolas in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety, fairly well-formed and fairly free of blemishes. Slight superficial defects in the skin and the ribs due to rubbing and bruises, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package. The total surface area affected shall not exceed 5%.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Size is determined by the weight of the carambola, in accordance with the following table:
Size Code Weight (in grams)
A 80 - 129
B 130 - 190
C > 190

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package (or in each lot for produce presented in bulk) for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

level=1

sections.title: SIZE TOLERANCES
sections.text: For “Extra” Class, 5%; and for Class I or Class II, 10 %; by number or weight of carambolas not satisfying the requirements as regards sizing, but falling within the class immediately above or below those indicated in Section 3.

sections.title: UNIFORMITY
sections.text: The contents of each package (or lot for produce presented in bulk)  must be uniform and contain only carambolas of the same origin, variety, quality and size . For “Extra” Class, colour and ripeness should be uniform. The visible part of the contents of the package (or lot for produce presented in bulk)  must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes carambolas which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above.  They must be reasonably well-formed and reasonably free of blemishes. Slight defects in the skin and the ribs due to rubbing and bruises, however, may be allowed, provided the carambolas retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation. The total surface area affected shall not exceed 10%.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text: Carambolas must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new 1, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Carambolas shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Recommended International Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995).

MARKING OR LABELLING
number=6
level=1

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the carambolas. Packages (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: CONSUMER PACKAGES
sections.text: In addition to the requirements of the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CODEX STAN 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS
sections.text: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment. For produce transported in bulk, these particulars must appear on a document accompanying the goods.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CODEX STAN 193-1995). The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Recommended International Code of Practice – General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1 -1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 53 -2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CAC/GL 21-1997).

sentences.original: This Standard applies to the fruit of commercial varieties of carambolas grown from Averrhoa carambola L., of the Oxalidaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.

components.scope_condition: In all classes, subject to special provisions for each class and tolerances allowed
requirements: sound (excluding produce affected by rotting or deterioration)
clean (practically free of visible foreign matter)
practically free of damage caused by pests
free of abnormal external moisture (excluding condensation after cold storage)
free of any foreign smell and/or taste
free of damage caused by low temperatures
sufficiently developed and display satisfactory ripeness

classification.justification: The sentence has a conditional antecedent followed by two coordinated modal expressions: obligation and permission. This combination with conditional modality and mixed normative statements does not strongly match known templates and requires a new structural pattern to capture the conditional plus dual modality coordination.
notes: The sentence blends condition, obligation, and permission within a single coordinated consequent, which is less common in typical regulatory templates and thus lacks close template match.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR CARAMBOLA (CODEX STAN 187-1993)
pages.summary: A página contém o título do documento e o cabeçalho da página. Possui a definição do produto e as disposições sobre qualidade, detalhadas em seções numeradas e hierarquizadas até o nível 3. Há continuidade na subseção 2.2.1 que segue para a próxima página.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 187
pages.summary: A página apresenta os títulos principais e suas subseções numeradas com seus respectivos textos associados. Identifica-se o documento CODEX STAN 187 e cabeçalho repetitivo com páginação. A estrutura hierárquica vai do nível 1 ao 3, com continuidade preservada para seções anteriores não presentes neste conteúdo.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 187
pages.summary: A página contém os títulos principais 5.2 PACKAGING e 6 MARKING OR LABELLING com suas subseções numeradas até o nível 3, apresentando texto associado bem delimitado. O conteúdo da seção 5.2 já continua da página anterior, e os demais títulos aparecem e finalizam nesta página, garantindo boa estrutura para RAG.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 187
pages.summary: A página contém as seções 7 CONTAMINANTS e 8 HYGIENE com suas subseções 7.1, 7.2, 8.1 e 8.2 bem identificadas. O texto explicativo está associado corretamente aos nós principais, enquanto as subseções atuam como organizadoras. Não há continuidade para outras páginas. Estrutura adequada para RAG.
